title: Naturalistic fMRI and MEG recordings during viewing of a reality TV show
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous naturalistic fMRI and MEG recordings from 60 participants (30 per modality) who watched a 25-minute excerpt from a Chinese reality TV show. The study captures neural responses during passive viewing and subsequent spontaneous mental replay, providing multimodal neuroimaging data for investigating naturalistic cognition and inter-subject correlation patterns. Both fMRI and MEG data are accompanied by anatomical MRI scans and behavioral comprehension assessments. Data from 6 MEG participants exhibited distinct power spectral density patterns and were excluded from inter-subject correlation and regression analyses (final MEG sample: 24 participants), though all datasets remain available in the repository.
methods_description: fMRI data were acquired on a 3.0 T Siemens Prisma scanner using T2-weighted echo planar imaging (63 interleaved axial slices, voxel size 2.5×2.5×2.5 mm, TR=2000 ms, TE=30 ms, flip angle=60°). MEG data were recorded using a 64-channel optically pumped magnetometer system (Quanmag) with 1000 Hz sampling rate and radial sensor orientation. Anatomical T1-weighted scans were acquired for both modalities. Participants viewed a naturalistic video stimulus followed by a comprehension quiz and 15 minutes of eyes-closed rest. fMRI preprocessing included skull stripping, motion correction, slice-timing correction, and spatial normalization to MNI space using fMRIPrep (v20.2.0).

funding_references.funder_name: Shanghai Key Laboratory of Brain-Machine Intelligence for Information Behavior
West China Hospital of Sichuan University Biomedical Research Ethics Committee
